code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test22-FEB-2024Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, significant proteinuria, glucosuria, bilirubinuria, and ketonuria, with low urine specific gravity and a low HbA1c. These findings suggest impaired renal function, likely nephrotic-range proteinuria, and possible tubular dysfunction. The presence of glucosuria with normal blood glucose and low HbA1c may indicate renal glycosuria. Urine abnormalities (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal) further support renal pathology. Recommend nephrology evaluation.
